description: Common xianxia cultivation terms. Provides consistent defaults across all xianxia novels. Book glossaries override these when a novel uses different translations.
resolution: Genre entries are defaults. Book-specific entries always win.

entries.note: First cultivation level. Also translated as 'Qi Condensation' or 'Qi Refining' in other novels.
Second cultivation level.
Third cultivation level. Forms the golden core.
Fourth cultivation level. Soul can exist independently of body.
Fifth cultivation level. Also 'Spirit Severing' in some novels.
Innate organ enabling cultivation. Quality determines talent.
Universal energy absorbed during cultivation. Also 'Qi' or 'Essence Energy'.
Energy center in the abdomen. Usually kept untranslated.
Sword controlled remotely via spiritual energy.
Magical tool or weapon. Also 'Magical Equipment' or 'Treasure'.
Paper or material inscribed with runes for magical effects.
Geometric arrangement of runes/people creating magical effects. Also 'Array'.
Cultivation school/organization.
Lightning/elemental trial when breaking through to higher levels.
Energy core formed during Core Formation stage.
Spiritual perception extending beyond normal senses. Also 'Spiritual Awareness'.
Ring containing dimensional storage space. Also 'Cosmos Ring'.
Crystallized spiritual energy used as currency/fuel. Also 'Crystal'.
Animals mutated/empowered by spiritual energy. Also 'Spirit Beast' or 'Monster'.
Cultivator using forbidden/dark techniques.
The act of surviving a heavenly tribulation to advance.
The Way. Fundamental concept of cultivation philosophy. Usually kept untranslated.
Highest cultivation achievement in classical xianxia.
Generic term for anyone who cultivates.
Cultivator without sect affiliation.
Cultivating with a partner. Can be sexual or purely spiritual depending on novel.
